topic: DeepSeek V4 Review 2026: 50x Cheaper Than GPT-5.4?

key_points: Compares DeepSeek V4 vs GPT-5.5 benchmarks and pricing
Discusses coding, reasoning, and real-world task performance
DeepSeek V4 Pro beats GPT-5.5 on coding benchmarks (LiveCodeBench 93.5%)
Covers V4-Pro vs V4-Flash use case breakdown
Highlights OpenAI-compatible API migration (2 lines of code)
1/6th cost of GPT-5.5 and Claude Opus 4.7 at standard pricing
V4-Pro costs 98% less than GPT-5.5 Pro
Highlights the MIT open-source license advantage
Detailed cost comparison across all token types (input, output, cached)
Real-world benchmark results across multiple tasks
Recommends use cases for each model

stats.fact: DeepSeek V4-Pro released April 24, 2026 under MIT open-source license
DeepSeek V4-Pro has 1.6 trillion total parameters with 49 billion active parameters per token (MoE architecture)
DeepSeek V4-Flash has 284 billion total parameters with 13 billion active parameters
Both V4-Pro and V4-Flash support a 1 million token context window
DeepSeek V4-Pro pricing: $1.74/M input tokens, $3.48/M output tokens (standard); cache-hit as low as $0.028/M
GPT-5.4 pricing: $2.50/M input, $15/M output (standard); GPT-5.4 Pro: $30/M input, $180/M output
GPT-5.5 pricing: $5.00/M input, $30.00/M output tokens
DeepSeek V4-Pro scores 93.5 on LiveCodeBench, outperforming GPT-5.5 (88.8%) and leading all models
V4-Pro Codeforces ELO: 3206 vs GPT-5.5 at 3168
V4-Pro SWE-bench Verified: 80.6%, just 0.2% behind Claude Opus 4.6 (80.8%)
V4-Pro Terminal-Bench 2.0: 67.9% vs Claude's 65.4%
DeepSeek V4-Flash is approximately 35x cheaper than GPT-5.5 on input tokens
V4-Pro uses only 27% of single-token inference FLOPs compared to DeepSeek V3.2
Both models trained on over 32 trillion tokens
DeepSeek censors ~85% of politically sensitive topics (Tiananmen Square, Taiwan, Uyghur camps, Xi Jinping criticism)
Cisco testing found 100% jailbreak success rate for DeepSeek — weakest safety guardrails among frontier models
DeepSeek banned from government devices in Texas, New York, Virginia, Florida and at the US federal level
NIST CAISI evaluation indicates DeepSeek V4 performs similarly to GPT-5 (released ~8 months prior)
Migration from OpenAI API to DeepSeek V4 API requires only 2 lines of code change (fully OpenAI-compatible)
V4-Pro costs approximately 70-80x less than GPT-5.4 Pro ($1.74 vs $30/M input tokens)

angles: The 50x cheaper claim: exactly how true is it? (V4-Flash vs Claude Opus 4.6 on cached pricing ≈ 50x; vs GPT-5.4 Pro ≈ 70-80x)
Open source disruption: MIT license + Hugging Face availability changes the calculus for self-hosted enterprise deployments
Coding crown: V4-Pro now leads LiveCodeBench and Codeforces, making it the de facto benchmark leader for developer-focused tasks
The China risk vs cost savings trade-off: censorship, security, and government bans vs dramatic cost reduction
V4-Pro vs V4-Flash decision framework: when the cheaper Flash variant is good enough and when you need Pro
Thinking mode integration: DeepSeek merged its R (reasoning) line into V4 — how switchable thinking modes work
1M token context: what this means for large codebase analysis and document processing use cases
Enterprise AI economics: At V4-Flash pricing, AI costs drop from budget concern to rounding error for most startups
keywords: DeepSeek V4 cost per million tokens
